comment_text.sn35.75:7.1: Here both sides of the upādāna metaphor are alive: Nibbana is the freedom of letting go all grasping at attachments; and it is the extinguishment of fuel that ignites the fire of suffering.

root_text.sn35.75:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu …pe… bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.75:1.2: “amukasmiṁ, bhante, vihāre aññataro bhikkhu navo appaññāto ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn35.75:1.3: Sādhu, bhante, bhagavā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.sn35.75:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā navavādañca sutvā gilānavādañca, “appaññāto bhikkhū”ti iti viditvā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.sn35.75:2.2: Addasā kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ.
root_text.sn35.75:2.4: Atha kho bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.75:2.7: Nisīdi bhagavā paññatte āsane. Nisajja kho bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.75:2.8: “kacci te, bhikkhu, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ, kacci dukkhā vedanā paṭikkamanti no abhikkamanti, paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.sn35.75:3.2: na kho maṁ, bhante, attā sīlato upavadatī”ti.
root_text.sn35.75:4.1: “No ce kira te, bhikkhu, attā sīlato upavadati, atha kiñca te kukkuccaṁ ko ca vippaṭisāro”ti?
root_text.sn35.75:5.1: “Na khvāhaṁ, bhante, sīlavisuddhatthaṁ bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.75:6.1: “No ce kira tvaṁ, bhikkhu, sīlavisuddhatthaṁ mayā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsi, atha kimatthaṁ carahi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mayā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsī”ti?
root_text.sn35.75:7.1: “Anupādāparinibbānatthaṁ khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn35.75:8.2: Sādhu kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu, anupādāparinibbānatthaṁ mayā dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāsi.
root_text.sn35.75:8.3: Anupādāparinibbānattho hi, bhikkhu, mayā dhammo desito.
root_text.sn35.75:11.9: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.75:13.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn35.75:15.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn35.75:17.1: “Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhu, sutavā ariyasāvako cakkhusmimpi nibbindati …pe…
root_text.sn35.75:17.5: Yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.75:17.6: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.75:17.7: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.
root_text.sn35.75:18.2: Attamano so bhikkhu bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandi.
root_text.sn35.75:18.3: Imasmiñca pana veyyākaraṇasmiṁ bhaññamāne tassa bhikkhussa anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuccīti.

translation_text.sn35.75:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and asked him,
translation_text.sn35.75:1.2: “Sir, in such and such a monastery there’s a mendicant who is junior and not well-known. He’s sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn35.75:1.3: Please go to him out of sympathy.”
translation_text.sn35.75:2.1: When the Buddha heard that the mendicant was junior and ill, understanding that he was not well-known, he went to him.
translation_text.sn35.75:2.2: That mendicant saw the Buddha coming off in the distance
translation_text.sn35.75:2.4: Then the Buddha said to that monk,
translation_text.sn35.75:2.5: “It’s all right, mendicant, don’t get up.
translation_text.sn35.75:2.6: There are some seats spread out, I will sit there.”
translation_text.sn35.75:2.7: He sat on the seat spread out and said to the mendicant,
translation_text.sn35.75:2.8: “I hope you’re keeping well, mendicant; I hope you’re all right. I hope that your pain is fading, not growing, that its fading is evident, not its growing.”
translation_text.sn35.75:3.1: “Sir, I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. …
translation_text.sn35.75:3.2: I have no reason to blame myself when it comes to ethical conduct.”
translation_text.sn35.75:4.1: “In that case, mendicant, why do you have remorse and regret?”
translation_text.sn35.75:5.1: “Because I understand that the Buddha has not taught the Dhamma merely for the sake of ethical purity.”
translation_text.sn35.75:6.1: “If that is so, what then do you understand to be the purpose of teaching the Dhamma?”
translation_text.sn35.75:7.1: “I understand that the Buddha has taught the Dhamma for the purpose of complete extinguishment without grasping fuel.”
translation_text.sn35.75:8.2: It’s good that you understand that I’ve taught the Dhamma for the purpose of complete extinguishment with no fuel for grasping.
translation_text.sn35.75:8.3: For that is indeed the purpose.
translation_text.sn35.75:9.2: Is the eye permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.75:11.9: The pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn35.75:13.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn35.75:15.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn35.75:15.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn35.75:17.1: “Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with the eye … ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.75:17.5: They grow disillusioned with the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.75:17.6: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn35.75:17.7: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
translation_text.sn35.75:18.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn35.75:18.2: Satisfied, that mendicant approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn35.75:18.3: And while this discourse was being spoken, the mind of that mendicant was freed from defilements by not grasping.
